block_message_in_dom: Copilot — Something went wrong. Please try again later.
console_error_observed: Uncaught (in promise) Error: Error parsing handshake response (Expected a handshake response from the server)
notes: Used pre-existing logged-in Chrome window — no Windows Hello PIN modal appeared (browser was already authenticated from prior session).
DevTools Network was already open with Preserve log + Disable cache enabled before submission.
Cleared network log immediately before submitting the PII prompt; substrate.office.com Chathub WSS handshake captured fresh.

Engine block confirmed in real time: WSS handshake completed (101 Switching Protocols) but server returned malformed/intercepted handshake response → SignalR client raised 'Error parsing handshake response' → UI rendered 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.'
17 substrate Chathub WSS connections observed during the 30s capture window (likely SignalR auto-reconnect attempts); 86 total WSS messages aggregate across all connections.
Original .har file is gitignored (contains live JWT auth_token); only sanitized extract_977.json is committed (auth_token redacted, cookies stripped, message bodies removed — only metadata kept).
This HAR replaces #898 (which was post-test, no Chathub WS visible) — Chathub WSS is now visible and captured during ACTIVE block window.
